questions.q: What is the concept of 'Right to Life' as enshrined in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948)?
questions.answer: The concept of 'Right to Life' in the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) implies that every individual has the inalienable right to live freely and safely, without any form of discrimination or violence.

questions.q: Identify one of the key features of the Right to Equality as mentioned in the Constitution of India.
Explain the difference between 'Right to Freedom' and 'Right against Exploitation' as per the Constitution of India.
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom (Article 19) guarantees the freedom of speech and expression, assembly, association, and movement, whereas the Right against Exploitation (Article 23-24) prohibits traffic in human beings, begar, and forced labour, and also protects children from trafficking and employment in hazardous occupations.

questions.q: Which of the following is a fundamental right under the Constitution of India?
Discuss the significance of the Right to Education as a fundamental right.
questions.answer: The Right to Education is a fundamental right that ensures every child has access to free and compulsory education up to the age of 14. This right is significant as it aims to eradicate illiteracy, promote social equality, and empower future generations.

questions.q: What is the significance of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) in terms of human rights?
questions.answer: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (1948) is significant as it is the first international document to enshrine the basic rights and freedoms of all individuals, setting a universal standard for human rights and providing a basis for national and international laws.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The Right to Information Act, 2005 is a legislation that provides citizens with the right to access information from the government under the ______ clause.
Explain the importance of the Right to Equality as a fundamental right.
questions.answer: The Right to Equality is a fundamental right that ensures equal treatment and opportunities for all individuals, regardless of their caste, creed, sex, or social status. This right is essential for promoting social justice, eliminating discrimination, and ensuring equal access to resources and opportunities.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a fundamental right under the Constitution of India?
Discuss the significance of the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression as a fundamental right.
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression is a fundamental right that ensures individuals can express their opinions, ideas, and thoughts freely, without fear of censorship or persecution. This right is essential for promoting democracy, facilitating public discourse, and holding those in power accountable.
